fact: July 27 is exactly 208 days into the year, which means there are only 157 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is peak summer heat time - the sun blazes down for about 15 hours a day in many places!
Cicadas are typically screaming their loudest around this date because the hot weather makes them super active and ready to find mates.
Fireflies often seem extra magical right now because July 27 falls during their peak flashing season when they're putting on their best light shows!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, this date marks the middle of winter - some areas might even have snow!
Sunflowers are usually turning their giant heads to follow the sun all day long around this time, like nature's own solar panels.
The moon phase around July 27 changes every year, but when it's a new moon on this date, you get the darkest summer nights perfect for stargazing.
This date falls smack in the middle of Leo season - the zodiac sign of the lion runs from July 23 to August 22.
In northern places like Alaska, the sun barely sets around this time, creating weird orange and pink skies that last for hours.
Mosquitoes are typically at their most annoying peak around July 27 because the hot, humid weather makes them breed like crazy.
Ocean water temperatures often hit their warmest points around this date in many coastal areas, making it perfect for swimming.
July 27 sits almost exactly 5 weeks after the summer solstice, when daylight hours start getting noticeably shorter each day.
